# Language Lineage

> An open dataset and interactive graph explorer documenting what programming languages are written in, how they are bootstrapped, and how they influenced each other.

## About

Language Lineage is a structured knowledge base of programming language implementation and influence relationships. It answers questions like "What is Python written in?", "How is Rust bootstrapped?", and "Which languages influenced JavaScript?".

The dataset covers 112 programming languages and tools, 347 documented relationships, and spans from 1949 (Fortran) to 2023.

## Dataset Facts

- **Total nodes:** 112 (98 language nodes, 14 tool nodes)
- **Total relationships:** 347
- **Date range:** 1949–2023
- **Evidence coverage:** 100% (all relationships have at least one evidence source)

## Relationship Types

| Type | Count |
|---|---|
| influenced | 189 |
| compiler written in | 78 |
| runtime written in | 56 |
| bootstrap written in | 14 |
| transpiled to | 8 |
| rewritten in | 2 |

## Key Pages

- Home: https://languagelineage.org/
- Graph Explorer: https://languagelineage.org/explore
- Dataset: https://languagelineage.org/dataset
- Sitemap: https://languagelineage.org/sitemap.xml

### Popular Language Pages

- Python: https://languagelineage.org/languages/python
- JavaScript: https://languagelineage.org/languages/javascript
- C: https://languagelineage.org/languages/c
- Rust: https://languagelineage.org/languages/rust
- Go: https://languagelineage.org/languages/go
- Java: https://languagelineage.org/languages/java
- C++: https://languagelineage.org/languages/cxx
- Haskell: https://languagelineage.org/languages/haskell

### Relationship Guides

- What is compiler bootstrapping: https://languagelineage.org/guides/what-is-compiler-bootstrapping
- What is self-hosting: https://languagelineage.org/guides/what-is-self-hosting
- Compiler vs interpreter vs runtime: https://languagelineage.org/guides/compiler-vs-interpreter-vs-runtime
- Programming language family tree: https://languagelineage.org/guides/programming-language-family-tree
- How Python is implemented: https://languagelineage.org/guides/how-python-is-implemented
- How Rust is bootstrapped: https://languagelineage.org/guides/how-rust-is-bootstrapped
- How JavaScript engines work: https://languagelineage.org/guides/how-javascript-engines-work
- GCC vs LLVM: https://languagelineage.org/guides/gcc-vs-llvm

## Data Schema

Each language node has: id, label, cluster, first_release_year, paradigm, typing, description.
Each relationship has: from_language, to_language, relationship type, start_year, end_year, confidence (0–1), evidence_source (URL), notes.

## Citation

If you reference Language Lineage data, please cite as:
"Language Lineage dataset (languagelineage.org). Accessed [date]."

## Source

Dataset source code and raw JSON: https://github.com/sanketmuchhala/LanguageLineage
